speaker: DOCTOR
text: Hello, Ms. Smith. Tell me what happened.

speaker: PATIENT
text: I passed out this morning. I was standing in the kitchen and the next thing I knew I was on the floor.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any warning before you passed out?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I felt lightheaded, a bit sweaty, and my vision went gray around the edges.

speaker: PATIENT
text: My husband said maybe ten seconds. I came to quickly.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any chest pain, palpitations, or shortness of breath before it happened?

speaker: PATIENT
text: No chest pain. My heart felt like it was racing for a second, but it was quick.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Did you bite your tongue or lose bladder control?

speaker: DOCTOR
text: You said your heart was racing — was that before or after you felt lightheaded?

speaker: PATIENT
text: Actually, thinking about it, the racing came first. Then the lightheaded feeling, then I went down.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Okay, that's important. Any similar episodes before?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I've felt dizzy when I stand up quickly, but I've never passed out before.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Any recent illness, fluid loss, or changes in medication?

speaker: PATIENT
text: I had a stomach bug two days ago, some diarrhea. I haven't been drinking much water.

speaker: PATIENT
text: I'm on metoprolol and hydrochlorothiazide for blood pressure. And a baby aspirin.

speaker: DOCTOR
text: I'd like to check orthostatic vitals and get an EKG.
